editionData.title: July 6, 2026: Families of children killed in strike on Iranian school join leader’s funeral; Turkey sees its status in NATO rise, thanks to its...
editionData.description: Global headlines: Families of children killed in strike on Iranian school join leader’s funeral (In Iran, the school deaths have become a symbol for U.S.-Israeli brutality and an unjust war. The Washington Post is reporting from the country under restricted conditions); Turkey sees its status in NATO rise, thanks to its weapons factories and Trump (A friendship between presidents Donald Trump and Recep Tayyip Erdogan, and a need for weapons production, has changed how other NATO countries treat Turkey); and Putin visits military installation, vowing to take more of Ukraine (The Russian president donned military fatigues as he tried to counter a narrative that Moscow is stumbling in its war after Ukrainian drone strikes set off an acute fuel shortage). Markets leaned higher, with 4 of 7 tracked indexes up; Euronext 100 Index up 0.91% was the largest move. Benchmarks tracked: S&P 500, NASDAQ Composite, Euronext 100 Index, and Public Joint-Stock Company Moscow Exchange MICEX-RTS plus 3 more indexes. Hacker News: GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra will be in Codex (@haider1 Ultra will be in codex); OpenPrinter (This is the homepage of the website); and Has_not_been_viewed_much (Museum APIs and the art that hasn). Product Hunt features DocsAlot (Documentation that works for both humans and AI systems), WorkBuddy (Produce sharpened results faster with a team of AI experts), and Toku Reader (Read & listen to native Japanese and Chinese, tap any word).
editionData.author: Sony Mathew

headlines.title: Families of children killed in strike on Iranian school join leader’s funeral
headlines.summary: In Iran, the school deaths have become a symbol for U.S.-Israeli brutality and an unjust war. The Washington Post is reporting from the country under restricted conditions.

headlines.title: Turkey sees its status in NATO rise, thanks to its weapons factories and Trump
headlines.summary: A friendship between presidents Donald Trump and Recep Tayyip Erdogan, and a need for weapons production, has changed how other NATO countries treat Turkey.

headlines.title: Putin visits military installation, vowing to take more of Ukraine
headlines.summary: The Russian president donned military fatigues as he tried to counter a narrative that Moscow is stumbling in its war after Ukrainian drone strikes set off an acute fuel shortage.

headlines.title: In Tehran, huge throngs mourn assassinated supreme leader Ali Khamenei
headlines.summary: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who ruled over the Islamic Republic of Iran for 37 years, was killed along with several family members in an airstrike in February.

headlines.title: On July Fourth, Pope Leo urges U.S. to welcome, protect and assist immigrants
headlines.summary: In Lampedusa, island gateway to Europe, the U.S.-born pope stressed human dignity and told America: "in every generation" immigrants "helped to shape the nation’s character.”

headlines.title: Is the US really preparing to drop Israel?
headlines.summary: With relations between Israel and the US fraught, some are speculating whether the special relationship may be at an end

headlines.title: How conflict minerals fuel war in eastern DR Congo amid US sanctions
headlines.summary: The move underscores growing scrutiny of the conflict mineral trade fuelling violence in eastern DR Congo.

headlines.title: Huge crowds in Tehran as Khamenei’s funeral procession gets under way
headlines.summary: The funeral procession is expected to last 10-12 hours and has drawn crowds of hundreds of thousands of mourners.

headlines.title: Venezuelan leader marks Independence Day with message of ‘no social unrest’
headlines.summary: Interim President Delcy Rodriguez once again defends her government's handling of the deadly earthquakes on June 24.

headlines.title: How a son rescued his father from the rubble of Venezuela’s earthquakes
headlines.summary: After the June 24 earthquakes, ex-firefighter Jesus Garcia found himself working to save his dad and younger brothers.

headlines.title: Trump won spending promises from NATO last year. This week, he'll try to enforce them
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump is heading to Ankara, Turkey, for the annual NATO summit. Last year, he pushed NATO allies to spend more on defense. This year, his mission is to try to enforce those pledges.

headlines.title: Mexico's World Cup run ends early again with loss to England at Estadio Azteca
headlines.summary: Playing at altitude with the passionate backing of 80,824 fans at Estadio Azteca, and with a man advantage for most of the second half, Mexico scored twice but could never equalize and lost 3-2.

headlines.title: Y si sí: the phrase uniting Mexico during the World Cup
headlines.summary: Mexico's World Cup run has inspired a phrase heard across the country: "Y si sí." Or in English: "What if we can?" Anamaria Sayre reports from Mexico City.

headlines.title: Thunderstorms, heat and wind will hamper efforts to contain Colorado wildfires
headlines.summary: Thunderstorms with high winds could make it more difficult to contain a massive wildfire that has scorched parts of southern Colorado.

headlines.title: National Guard troops fatally shoot a man in downtown Memphis
headlines.summary: Memphis police say National Guard troops fired their weapons in the early morning hours of July 5, killing a man who was armed with a handgun. State investigators will handle the case.

headlines.title: German far-right rally sparks anger
headlines.summary: The far-right Alternative for Germany party, or AfD, held its biennial conference in the eastern city of Erfurt over the weekend, making plans for state elections in the fall, against a backdrop of mass public protests in the city.

headlines.title: Tariffs dim fireworks at celebrations
headlines.summary: As cities across the United States marked the nation's 250th birthday, the celebrations might have looked different this year, with rising costs casting a shadow over the longstanding tradition of the Independence Day fireworks display.

headlines.title: Trump holds calls with Putin, Zelensky
headlines.summary: MOSCOW — US President Donald Trump spoke with his Russian and Ukrainian counterparts Vladimir Putin and Volodymyr Zelensky on Saturday to discuss the conflict in Ukraine ahead of a NATO summit in Ankara, the Kremlin and Zelensky said.

headlines.title: Wake-up call for the American Dream
headlines.summary: Achieving the American Dream may take the painstaking toil of generations, yet it takes only two bills to shatter it: a health insurance premium and a college tuition statement.

headlines.title: China, Finland agree to strengthen cooperation, jointly address challenges
headlines.summary: Finnish President Alexander Stubb and visiting Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi pledged on Sunday to strengthen cooperation and coordination and to jointly address challenges.

headlines.title: Jan Suraaj Party fields Prashant Kishor for Bankipore Assembly Bypoll
headlines.summary: Jan Suraaj announces Prashant Kishor as its candidate for the Bankipore Assembly bypoll following Nitin Nabin's Rajya Sabha election.

headlines.title: Government withdraws emergency gas curbs imposed during Hormuz disruption as LNG supplies normalise
headlines.summary: Government lifts emergency gas supply curbs as LNG shipments normalize following the ceasefire in the West Asia conflict.

headlines.title: Mumbai-Pune train services suspended after landslides in Karjat-Lonavala section | Monsoon LIVE updates
headlines.summary: Weather today LIVE: Get updates on Mumbai rain, traffic, train services and IMD weather forecasts as heavy showers continue unabated across the city.

headlines.title: Madhya Pradesh Waqf Board reorganised, Hindu members included
headlines.summary: Madhya Pradesh Waqf Board reorganization includes Hindu members, marking a historic first under the Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025.

headlines.title: We are prepared for 2-D Bills — Delimitation and Dismissal: Jairam Ramesh
headlines.summary: Jairam Ramesh asserts Congress is ready to block the Delimitation and Dismissal Bills in the upcoming Monsoon Session.

headlines.title: Australia PM Albanese apologises for crude Kylie Minogue remark
headlines.summary: Australian Prime Minister ​Anthony Albanese apologised on Monday after making ‌a crude remark about singer Kylie Minogue in a comedy podcast interview, prompting outrage from women's rights ​groups and opposition lawmakers.

headlines.title: Asian shares slip as earnings loom, oil weighed by supply
headlines.summary: Asian share markets sputtered on Monday as caution took hold ahead of a crucial earnings season for the AI sector, while the potential for increased supply weighed on oil prices and ​promised relief from inflationary pressures.

headlines.title: South Korea's SK Hynix launching $28 billion US listing to ride global AI wave
headlines.summary: The South Korean chipmaker is capitalising on the global artificial intelligence boom with one of the world's largest new share sales.

headlines.title: Morning Bid: Samsung to serve chip taster for earnings feast
headlines.summary: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Wayne Cole.

headlines.title: Russian attack on Kyiv kills nine, hits apartment buildings
headlines.summary: Rescue crews were pulling residents from buildings struck by the overnight barrage.

hackerNews.title: Does code cleanliness affect coding agents? A controlled minimal-pair study
hackerNews.summary: As autonomous coding agents see rapid adoption, their evaluation has primarily focused on task completion rates holding the target codebase fixed. This leaves a critical question unanswered: does the structural and stylistic quality, or ``cleanliness'' of the underlying code affect an agent's ability to navigate and modify it? To isolate the effect of code cleanliness from agent capability, we introduce an evaluation protocol built around minimal pairs: repositories that match on architecture, dependencies, and external behaviour, but differ on static-analysis rule violations and cognitive complexity. The pairs are constructed in both directions, by agent pipelines that either degrade a clean repository or clean a messy one. We author 33 tasks across six such pairs, evaluated through hidden tests at the application's public surface. Across 660 trials with Claude Code, code cleanliness does not change the agent's pass rate. However, it substantially alters the agent's operational footprint: agents working on cleaner code use 7 to 8% fewer tokens and reduce file revisitations by 34%. Our findings suggest that traditional maintainability principles remain highly relevant in the era of AI-driven development, shaping the computational cost and navigational efficiency of coding agents. Code cleanliness joins model choice, harness, and prompting as a factor that materially affects agent behaviours.
